prompt: A HarborBite Offline Restaurant Ordering & Risk Management system with main features including on-premise menu discovery, promotion-aware checkout, secure in-store payment capture, and end-to-end fraud controls. Regular Guests browse a tablet/kiosk interface built with Laravel + Livewire, where search supports keyword queries, price-range filtering, category attributes (e.g., “gluten-free,” “spicy level,” “contains nuts”), and sorting by relevance, newest, or price; recent searches are stored locally and admins can pin up to 20 trending terms per location. Sensitive or abnormal terms (profanity, scam keywords, or staff-defined banned words) are blocked with a clear inline message and a suggestion to refine the query. Guests build an order by adding/removing items, editing quantities, selecting flavor preferences, and leaving short notes capped at 140 characters; the cart always shows an itemized subtotal, estimated sales tax, and a traceable discount breakdown. Promotions are applied automatically at checkout using “best offer wins” logic, such as 10% off orders over $30.00, $5.00 off over $50.00, buy-one-get-one for specific SKUs, or 50% off the second item, with mutual exclusions enforced (e.g., BOGO cannot stack with percentage discounts) and time windows shown in local time (e.g., 03/27/2026 5:00 PM–8:00 PM). Staff roles include Cashier (confirm and serve), Kitchen (prepare), Manager (override and incident handling), and Administrator (configuration and analytics). The order lifecycle is visible in real time as Pending Confirmation, In Preparation, Served, Settled, or Canceled, with immediate UI feedback on conflicts (e.g., “item changed by staff”) to prevent double edits. The backend uses Laravel to expose offline REST-style endpoints consumed by Livewire for decoupled page updates, with PostgreSQL persisting orders, line items, promotions, risk events, and analytics events locally on the site server. Order updates follow a strict state machine with optimistic version control; any state-changing request must include the last-known version and will be rejected if stale, requiring a refresh before retry. Risk Control & Anti-Fraud is enforced through configurable rules: per-device and per-IP rate limiting (e.g., max 10 registrations/hour, max 30 checkout attempts/10 minutes), CAPTCHA after 5 failed logins or 3 rapid cart re-pricings within 60 seconds, and step-up verification for critical actions (manager PIN required to cancel after “In Preparation,” override discount over $20.00, or mark as “Settled” when payment is ambiguous). Device fingerprinting uses a locally generated identifier derived from user agent, screen traits, and a salted hash stored on the server; blacklists/whitelists can target device IDs, IP ranges, or usernames, and every rule hit writes an immutable hit log. Payments and fund security are implemented in an offline mode: the system records payment intents, accepts locally entered payment confirmations, signs sensitive parameters with HMAC to prevent tampering and replay (nonce + 5-minute expiry), and runs idempotent processing on any repeated callbacks/imports using a unique payment reference. Abnormal payment-state repair supports reconciling “paid but not settled” orders through a manager-reviewed queue that creates incident tickets and risk alerts. Analytics & observability are fully local: DAU, GMV, conversion, funnel, and retention are computed from event tracking stored in PostgreSQL, while API performance, error rates, and trace IDs are written to rotating local logs with threshold-based alerts displayed on an admin dashboard; sensitive fields (passwords, manager PINs) are hashed, and customer notes are encrypted at rest with key rotation stored only on-premise.
